preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an ai-first, intent-driven design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Opens with a sharp, direct hero statement that frames MODE as the AI-first, intent-driven design system built for startups — gives the Mover immediate context and confirms they're in the right place without lingering.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. For a Mover archetype, this is kept lean — a tight set of metrics or a single compelling case study data point that eliminates doubt fast and justifies the conversion ahead.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. Presents pricing and plan comparison clearly so the Mover can self-select quickly — no ambiguity, just the information needed to commit.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. Direct CTA with a clear commitment prompt — sign up or start a trial. Mover archetype demands a frictionless, confident action moment with no hedging.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat as required. Provides secondary paths — contact sales, explore docs, or revisit feature details — catching anyone who didn't convert on the primary CTA without cluttering the main flow.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Opens with a sharp, direct hero statement that frames MODE as the AI-first, intent-driven design system built for startups — gives the Mover immediate context and confirms they're in the right place without lingering.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. For a Mover archetype, this is kept lean — a tight set of metrics or a single compelling case study data point that eliminates doubt fast and justifies the conversion ahead.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. Presents pricing and plan comparison clearly so the Mover can self-select quickly — no ambiguity, just the information needed to commit.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. Direct CTA with a clear commitment prompt — sign up or start a trial. Mover archetype demands a frictionless, confident action moment with no hedging.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion page requires minimal variant to 'strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' — the persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element and must remain focused.

Conversion funnel stage demands text-only variant: 'slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.' Mover archetype reinforces suppression of secondary elements.

slots.headline: Your design system finally knows why.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, copy, and visual emphasis to intent — no manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. Runs in your codebase.

page.reasoning: Mover archetype with decision-stage context requires suppression of sources and headlines per variant notes: 'Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact.' The 3-stat default is preferred for clean, decisive presentation.

stats.label: Archetype, stage, intent, context — resolved per component
Variants generated from a single brief

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion requires clarity on recommended plan. Mover variant 'strips description; price and CTA only' — the highlighted variant with stripped detail creates decisive conversion focus.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Yours forever — no subscription, no lock-in.
features: Bring your own Anthropic API key

page.reasoning: Mover archetype at decision stage demands 'strong headline, single CTA, no subhead' — minimal variant avoids friction and secondary messaging that competes with the conversion action.

slots.headline: Stop configuring. Start shipping.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. No subscription. Running in under an hour.

page.reasoning: Conversion page footer should 'recapture departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows' via the with-cta variant, optimizing the final moment before exit.
